data.title: As a developer, the large-commit review path classifies my commit's transformation pattern before routing to the appropriate review strategy
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Create REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md implementing stratified 7-file sampling, haiku pattern-extraction, >=5/7 consensus evaluation, routing to the confirmed-refactor or fallback path, and review-recording integration so commits reviewed via this path can proceed through the pre-commit review gate.

**Why**: This is the core of the large-refactor review path. Classification must happen before dispatch to enable cost-efficient haiku batching for true refactors. Without this file, the diversion gate from S1 has nowhere to route.

**Scope**:
- IN: REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md creation, 7-file stratified sampling (prioritizing highest-line-count files and directory spread), haiku pattern-extraction call, consensus evaluation (>=5/7 → confirmed-refactor, <5/7 → fallback), <7 eligible files → low confidence → fallback, routing signal to confirmed-refactor or fallback path, review-recording step (record-review.sh invocation with valid REVIEWER_HASH and DIFF_HASH) so the pre-commit review gate passes
- OUT: confirmed-refactor review agents (Story S3), MODEL_OVERRIDE fallback logic (Story S4), configuration documentation (Story S6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md exists and implements stratified sampling of 7 files (prioritizing highest-line-count files and directory spread) from the diff's eligible file set
  ← Satisfies: "REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE identifies confirmed refactors by sampling 7 stratified files and requiring >=5/7 to show a consistent transformation pattern"
- When this story is complete, >=5/7 sampled files agreeing on a transformation pattern results in high-confidence routing to the confirmed-refactor path; fewer than 5/7 results in low-confidence routing to the fallback path
  ← Satisfies: "REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE identifies confirmed refactors by sampling 7 stratified files and requiring >=5/7 to show a consistent transformation pattern"
- When this story is complete, if fewer than 7 files are eligible for sampling after .test-index and binary/generated exclusions, sampling is skipped and the commit routes to the fallback path with low-confidence status
  ← Satisfies: "If fewer than 7 files are eligible for sampling after .test-index and binary/generated file exclusions, sampling is skipped and the commit is treated as low confidence, routing directly to the fallback path"
- When this story is complete, REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md includes a review-recording step that writes review-status via record-review.sh (with valid REVIEWER_HASH and DIFF_HASH), enabling the pre-commit review gate to pass for commits reviewed via this path
  ← Satisfies: "The final findings file is identical in schema to the standard review path output" (convergence requirement — commits must be able to proceed)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Pattern extraction uses a haiku sub-agent — ensure REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md can be exercised in tests with a mock or stub for the haiku call; the consensus evaluation logic should be unit-testable independently of live agent dispatch
- [Reliability] Pattern extraction failure (timeout, malformed output) must fall back gracefully to low-confidence routing rather than blocking the review workflow entirely
- [Maintainability] Define a contract (inline schema or interface doc) for the extracted-pattern artifact that S3's haiku batch agents will consume; specify format, required fields (e.g., transformation_description, before_pattern, after_pattern), and how the pattern is passed to batch agents — this contract must be established in this story so S3 can rely on it

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
